title: The Acorn
body:
The acorn falls from the tree,
rolling down the hill,
quiet, deliberate,
gathering the weight of each passing moment.

At the base, it rests,
buried beneath layers of time and soil.
In stillness, it cracks open,
roots reaching deep, unseen,
while a fragile stem breaks through the surface,
seeking light.

No longer small,
no longer hidden,
it becomes what it was meant to be—
an oak, standing firm,
tall against the sky,
with the memory of its fall
still deep within its core.
